トピック.授業計画1:
<Presentation> Students will learn and practice presentation skills and present on policy-based topics in pairs and small groups.
<Reading> Students will complete reading skill and comprehension activities around a variety of texts as well as extensive reading quizzes and discussions.
<Seminar> Students will develop an ability to discuss a topic for at least 5 minutes. Topics will require students to relate their lives to test preparation materials.
<Writing> Students will complete several paragraph-length assignments. There will also be tests to help students reviewtheir learning and to check on their progress.
トピック.授業外学習2:
<Presentation> Students will be required to work individually or as a group to prepare and practice their presentations and to do self reﬂections after the presentations have been completed.
<Reading> Students will practice reading comprehension and reading skills through texts, read graded readers, and prepare for quizzes and tests.
<Seminar>Students will engage with test materials for meaning and structure. Students study vocabulary, questions, and skills before speaking tests.
<Writing> Students complete writing activities, study two-supporting idea paragraphs and practice the writing process in their own work. They are also required to revise their assignments based on teacher feedback. They will also need to prepare for quizzes, and review the course materials on paragraph organization for their writing tests.

教科書/Required texts0: <Seminar> <Writing> <Presentation>Materials will be available on LUNA. You should bring your materials to class on your device (computer / tablet) or print and bring paper copies. If you do not bring a device, you should also bring a dictionary to class. In addition, you will need to purchase an Xreading card.
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: Each class subject is arranged on the curriculum based on the Diploma Policy (DP) and Curriculum Policy (CP) established by each faculty and graduate school. By studying this course with DP/CP in mind, you will develop the abilities expected by each faculty and graduate school. The DP, CP, and curriculum table (list of classes, etc.) for each faculty/graduate school are posted in the link below, so please use them as a reference for your study plan. https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1 *Link to kwic (only current students can view)
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note <Presentation> Students will be assessed on Presentation delivery, visual aid quality & quality of planning and reﬂection.<Reading> Students will be assessed using quizzes, unit tests, discussions, extensive reading, and class participation.<Seminar> Students are assessed on this course through unit discussion tests, test preparation materials, and article-related discussion preparation materials.<Writing> Students are assessed on the drafts of essays they write, quizzes, in-class writing activities.

name: 【150】English Communication III　Ａ／English Communication III

成績評価備考:
備考　Note
<Presentation> Students will be assessed on Presentation delivery, visual aid quality & quality of planning and reﬂection.
<Reading> Students will be assessed using quizzes, unit tests, discussions, extensive reading, and class participation.
<Seminar> Students are assessed on this course through unit discussion tests, test preparation materials, and article-related discussion preparation materials.
